reverseStrings.name: Reverse Strings
reverseStrings.description: Reverse a string so that it reads from right to left instead of left to right.

reverseEmptyString.name: Empty string
reverseEmptyString.description: An empty string should return an empty string when reversed.

reverseSentencePunctuation.name: Sentence with punctuation
reverseSentencePunctuation.description: Reverse a sentence with punctuation 'I'm hungry!'

reversePalindrome.name: Palindrome
reversePalindrome.description: A palindrome remains the same when reversed.

reverseEmojiRainbowFlag.name: Rainbow Flag
reverseEmojiRainbowFlag.description: Make a rainbow flag by reversing.

reverseByHand.question: How would I reverse a word by hand?
reverseByHand.answer: You'd read it left-to-right but write it right-to-left. Equivalently, you'd take each letter in turn and put it in front of what you've written so far.

mapToCode.question: How does that map to code?
mapToCode.answer: Loop through the input string character by character. For each character, build the result by prepending. That means putting it at the FRONT of your result, not the back.

prepend.question: How do I 'prepend' instead of append?
prepend.answer: With the `+` operator, prepending is just a matter of order. Instead of `result + char` (which appends), use `char + result` (which prepends).
